title: Hierarchical (P,Q,T) map-reduce merge; merged result bit-identical to single-host
context: bigger-pi is an open, distributed, verification-first effort to compute more digits of pi on donated compute; the public good is the open, reproducible, independently-verified toolchain, chunk-coordination protocol, and provenance ledger. The map-reduce merge combines accepted (P,Q,T) triples and must reproduce the single-host result bit-identically; this equivalence is the correctness contract of the whole distributed design. It depends on chunk-007.
objective: Implement the hierarchical (P,Q,T) map-reduce merge whose merged result is bit-identical to a single-host computation of the same range.
acceptanceCriteria: Hierarchically merges accepted (P, Q, T) triples using the associative combine; the merged root triple and assembled result are bit-identical to a single-host computation of the same range (asserted in CI).
Merge is order-independent for a fixed partition (associativity verified by test).
output: A PR adding the hierarchical (P,Q,T) map-reduce merge with bit-identical-to-single-host assertions.
